extraction.one_line_summary: CICCONE attacked early on the Vedonnia climb (~40km out), shed CARTHY and BUITRAGO on the final 22km ascent, winning by over a minute.
tactical_patterns: CICCONE launched decisive move on penultimate climb (Vedonnia) before final 22km ascent, not waiting for traditional finale positioning.
BOUWMAN sacrificed stage win to take mountains jersey on first climb, revealing KOM competition tactical priority over stage hunting.
MARTIN escaped GC group mid-stage to limit losses and salvage top-10, demonstrating damage control tactics after previous day collapse.
INEOS controlled calmly despite 30-rider break because no GC threats escaped, showing numeric breakaway size irrelevant without threat calculation.

rider_signals.CICCONE Giulio: positive: First victory since pre-pandemic after years of poor form, exploited steep early gradients on 22km finale to split group
rider_signals.BUITRAGO Santiago: positive: Followed CICCONE initially on Vedonnia showing climbing strength, but cracked on sustained final ascent revealing weakness on long gradual drags
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: positive: Fought back after being dropped to rejoin lead group, demonstrating unusual resilience but ultimately lacked punch when CICCONE attacked
rider_signals.MARTIN Guillaume: positive: Attacked from GC group mid-stage for damage limitation, moving into top-10 by taking 2+ minutes on rivals
rider_signals.BOUWMAN Koen: neutral: Attacked early for KOM points then sat up, confirming mountains jersey as primary objective over stage wins
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: Crashed early but rejoined unscathed, INEOS controlled passively suggesting confidence or fatigue in team
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Juan Pedro: negative: Lost pink jersey after 10 days, implicit weakness as CARAPAZ took lead
